title: Station homecoming evolution pass
summary: Make return-to-station feel more earned through calm visible changes instead of a larger dashboard or copy stack.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Station return after major filed beats visibly changes in one compact seam and remains readable at 256x160.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Extract or harden the station homecoming state resolver and wire one additional non-sill visual payoff seam.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_342_target: Add the smallest station-owned homecoming state seam, consume the reviewed lane-2 WELCOME BACK copy helper conditionally, and expose one non-sill seam key for later lane-3 visual accent work.
existing_seams: resolveFieldStationOpenState(...) already emits arrivalMode homecoming only when opening the station mutates progress state
fieldStationArrivalMode is stored in game.ts and exposed through render_game_to_text().fieldStation.arrivalMode
field-station-homecoming-shell already consumes arrivalMode for the transient brace pulse
resolveFieldStationHomecomingCopy(...) is reviewed and intentionally unwired until lane 1 provides the station seam
main_acceptance: consume resolveFieldStationHomecomingCopy(...) only through a station-owned homecoming state seam
keep homecoming copy conditional on arrivalMode === homecoming so it is not an always-on station subtitle
expose fieldStation.homecoming with label, text, and requestId during earned homecoming opens and null on calm reopens
add or extend pure tests for default, earned, and repeated/calm station open states
add focused runtime-smoke coverage proving a representative earned station return exposes the homecoming state and a second open clears it
give lane 3 one non-sill seam key or flag to consume later without drawing the lane-3 visual accent yet
do not change save schema, route definitions, route filing behavior, support notices, geometry, science copy, lane-2 copy text, new station pages, or broad station layout
non_goals: new dashboard, page, card stack, or permanent station subtitle
committed screenshots or browser proof unless runtime rendering changes require it
lane-3 upper-frame visual accent implementation before ECO-20260420-critic-342
lane-4 route-notice lifecycle changes before ECO-20260420-critic-342
edits to lane-2 copy text unless a concrete bug appears

main_result.summary: Added a station-owned homecoming state resolver that consumes the lane-2 WELCOME BACK copy helper only when arrivalMode is homecoming, exposes fieldStation.homecoming in debug state, shows one transient compact header-line during earned returns, and gives lane 3 a non-drawing homecoming memory seam.
guardrails_kept: no route definitions or route filing behavior changes
no geometry, science copy, or lane-2 copy text changes
no new station page, panel, card stack, or broad station layout change

confirmed: station-owned resolver consumes the lane-2 copy helper instead of letting rendering or route code import it directly
fieldStation.homecoming is conditional on arrivalMode homecoming plus a completed filed-progress milestone and clears on calm reopen
visible copy stays to one transient existing header-line without a new station page, panel, card stack, or permanent subtitle
homecomingMilestoneRequestId and hasHomecomingMemory are exposed as a non-drawing lane-3 seam
save schema, route definitions, route filing behavior, support notices, geometry, science copy, and lane-2 copy text stayed unchanged
critic_result.watch_item: If later work needs to distinguish route-filed returns from nursery or ledger homecoming returns, extend resolveFieldStationOpenState(...) with a narrow reason/key instead of adding parallel homecoming state.

confirmed: hasHomecomingFrameAccent is derived from the reviewed station-owned homecomingMilestoneRequestId seam and exposed through fieldStation.backdropAccent
visible treatment stays to two tiny upper-frame cap beads without lower-sill growth, side-gutter bulk, another full-width lintel, panel, or text
growth accent state still ignores homecomingMilestoneRequestId
archived High Pass keeps its existing late-season lintel while homecoming only adds cap memory
focused runtime and browser proof confirm the representative homecoming return exposes the cap without lighting right brace, center tie, or late-season lintel
critic_result.watch_item: If playtesting says the cap is too subtle, tune contrast or placement inside this same cap seam instead of adding more lower sill, side gutter, lintel, copy, or panels.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Write a tiny homecoming line family that acknowledges filed progress without repeating route notes or adding a new panel.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_343_target: Add a tiny copy-only homecoming line resolver/data bank that acknowledges filed progress from existing completedFieldRequestIds, with direct tests and no station wiring until lane 1 lands the homecoming resolver seam.

milestones.text: Coast line filed. The station holds one shore-to-forest thread.
Coast and ridge filed. The board reads like one longer path.
Edge line filed. Coast, ridge, and low fell now connect.
Root Hollow filed. The season reaches from shore to upper run.
Season Threads filed. The station can rest before High Pass.
High Pass filed. Treeline stone and talus close the field arc.

non_goals: station rendering, station state, route controller behavior, route-board layout, save/schema changes, geometry, source-ledger changes for non-science station copy, new panels, new notice priority, or wider station subtitles
repeating route notes, clue lists, or filed-note synthesis copy from packet 133
verification: direct focused tests for the new copy resolver/data bank
`npm run build` if the shared tree allows TypeScript build verification

main_result.summary: Added a copy-only field-station homecoming resolver/data bank with six filed-progress milestones, compact budgets, strongest-applicable resolution from completedFieldRequestIds, and direct tests.

confirmed: copy-only helper reads existing completedFieldRequestIds and stays unwired until lane 1 provides the station seam
strongest-applicable filed milestone selection is test-backed
label/text budgets and clue-list avoidance are test-backed
no station rendering, station state, route controller behavior, route-board layout, save schema, geometry, or new UI panel changed
critic_result.residual_risk: When lane 1 consumes the helper, the line should stay conditional on the earned homecoming state instead of becoming an always-on station subtitle.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Design the visual accent family for the new station seam and prove it does not crowd the existing sill/gutter accents.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

main_result.summary: Added one tiny upper-frame homecoming memory cap driven by lane 1's reviewed fieldStation.homecoming / homecomingMilestoneRequestId seam, exposed as hasHomecomingFrameAccent in the existing backdropAccent debug state.
guardrails_kept: no route, station, save, copy, support-notice, science, journal, panel, or station-page state added
no lower-sill growth, side-gutter bulk, or second full lintel
archived High Pass keeps its existing lintel while homecoming only adds frame-cap beads

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Ensure route filing and support notices hand off cleanly into homecoming without competing toasts or stale Today labels.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_345_target: After lane 1's homecoming seam review clears, add the smallest route-notice/replay lifecycle proof so filed-route notices, support-selection notices, and world-map Today labels hand off cleanly around station homecoming.
scout_refinement.dependency_note: Keep ECO-20260420-main-345 blocked until ECO-20260420-critic-342 clears; lane 2's copy helper review has already cleared and should be respected rather than edited.
main_acceptance: opening a homecoming station state does not let stale FIELD STATION, support-selection, or route-replay notices reappear later
filing a ready Route v2 note from the station keeps the filed-route notice as the only route notice generated by that action
support selection remains persisted but does not replace the filed-route notice in the same station interaction
world-map routeMarkerLocationId, routeReplayLabel, active outing, and journal field-request state clear for filed treeline-high-pass or hand off to the next real outing for earlier route filings
no station layout, homecoming copy, station shell visuals, route definitions, save schema, geometry, broad onboarding copy, or science content changes
verification: focused runtime-smoke station filing/homecoming notice handoff slice
`npm test -- src/test/field-requests.test.ts` if the pure route-state matrix is touched
`npm test -- src/test/field-notices.test.ts` if a notice-priority helper is added
`npm run build` for runtime TypeScript changes

main_result.summary: Added tiny notice-lifecycle helpers and runtime wiring so earned homecoming station opens clear stale non-filed notices, while filed-route notices remain canonical and cannot be replaced by support-selection copy during the same station interaction.
guardrails_kept: consumed lane 1's reviewed station-owned homecoming seam
no station layout, station shell visual, route definition, save schema, geometry, broad onboarding copy, or science content changes
verification: `npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "clears stale station guidance notices"`
`npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "lets hand lens prefer talus cushion pocket"`

confirmed: earned homecoming station opens clear stale non-filed notices through lane 1's reviewed station-owned arrivalMode seam
filed-route notices stay canonical and cannot be replaced by non-filed support-selection copy during the same station interaction
filed treeline-high-pass clears active request, route marker, replay label, and journal field-request state
homecoming copy, station layout, station shell visuals, route definitions, save schema, geometry, broad onboarding copy, and science content stayed unchanged
critic_result.residual_risk: Filed-route notices are intentionally preserved on homecoming opens; if a later route replay pass creates a truly stale filed-route notice, add a narrow age/source check instead of clearing all filed-route notices.
verification: `npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "clears stale station guidance notices"`
`npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "lets hand lens prefer talus cushion pocket"`

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
